subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 18
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, community, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is known for its black and white stripes?
questions.explanation: Zebras are African equids known for their distinctive black-and-white striped coats.
questions.hint: They look like a horse but with patterns.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their characteristics.

questions.q: What is the capital city of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo is the bustling capital city of Japan.

questions.q: Which of these is a gas that we need to breathe in to survive?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is the gas that humans and many animals need to breathe in order to live.
questions.hint: It is all around us in the air.

questions.q: What is the name of the planet we live on?
questions.explanation: Earth is the third planet from the Sun and the only known place in the universe to harbor life.
questions.hint: It is also known as the 'Blue Planet'.
questions.objective: Understand basic facts about the solar system.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy habit for your body?
questions.explanation: Regular exercise is important for maintaining a healthy heart and strong muscles.
questions.hint: Think about moving your body to get your heart rate up.

questions.q: Who is the community helper that helps keep our teeth healthy?
questions.explanation: A dentist is a health professional who specializes in the care of teeth and gums.
questions.hint: They use tools to clean your teeth.

questions.q: What do we call the water that falls from the sky?
questions.explanation: Rain is liquid water in the form of droplets that have condensed from atmospheric water vapor and then fall under gravity.

questions.q: Which continent is the Sahara Desert located in?
questions.explanation: The Sahara Desert is the largest hot desert in the world, covering much of North Africa.
questions.hint: It is a very large continent.

questions.q: What is the force that acts between two surfaces that are sliding across each other?
questions.explanation: Friction is the force that opposes the motion of one surface over another.

questions.q: Which of these is a type of musical instrument?
questions.explanation: A violin is a string instrument that is played with a bow.
